# CTRubyPosition.count

**Case**

A constant that accounts for all ruby positions during ruby annotation creation.

## Declaration

```swift
case count
```

## Discussion

When you create a ruby annotation using [doc://com.apple.coretext/documentation/CoreText/CTRubyAnnotationCreate(_:_:_:_:)], use this constant to allocate an array of [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFString] texts that contains a sufficient number of elements for each [doc://com.apple.coretext/documentation/CoreText/CTRubyPosition].

## Constants

- **CTRubyPosition.before**: The ruby text is positioned before the base text, appearing above horizontal text and to the right of vertical text.
- **CTRubyPosition.after**: The ruby text is positioned after the base text, appearing below horizontal text and to the left of vertical text.
- **CTRubyPosition.interCharacter**: The ruby text is positioned to the right of the base text, regardless of whether it’s horizontal or vertical.
- **CTRubyPosition.inline**: The ruby text follows the base text with no special styling.
